mai-stand-light-punch.name: Standing Light Punch
mai-stand-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; useful in combos due to its extra 1f hitstun over 2LP (combos naturally into 214LP/MP, 236KK, and has more utility in CH/PC/DR routes)

mai-stand-medium-punch.name: Standing Medium Punch
mai-stand-medium-punch.notes_sc: Great range for a cancelable poke; solid hitbox priority; important combo tool from Punish Counter 2LP (works from max range); has an extended hurtbox on whiff until the end of recovery

mai-stand-heavy-punch.name: Standing Heavy Punch
notes: Forces the opponent into a standing position
mai-stand-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Very long range for a cancelable poke; great for combos and whiff punishing; combos from PC/DR 5LP for optimal conversions; slightly less pushback on Counter-hit/Punish Counter (makes some ranged 214P cancels more reliable)

mai-stand-light-kick.name: Standing Light Kick
mai-stand-light-kick.notes_sc: Very short range (only combos naturally from point blank 5LP/2LP); only useful for 5LK~LK~LK Target Combo

mai-stand-medium-kick.name: Standing Medium Kick
mai-stand-medium-kick.notes_sc: Solid poke with good hitbox priority that can lead to combos with CH/PC/DR; links consistently into SA2 when used as a whiff punish; Mai lifts up her front hurtbox on frames 4-17 making it useful vs. low attacks

mai-stand-heavy-kick.name: Standing Heavy Kick
notes: Produces a crumple stun on Punish Counter, and +22 frame advantage
mai-stand-heavy-kick.notes_sc: 3f extra recovery on whiff; Great range for a safe poke; Mai lifts up her leg hurtbox on frames 10-16 making it decent vs. low attacks; grounded Punish Counter causes a crumple state (opponent is airborne for all follow-up hits); airborne Punish Counter puts opponents into limited juggle state; extends a hurtbox 1f before active that is vulnerable to projectiles; extends a hurtbox at mid-height for the first 17 recovery frames, making it more whiff punishable by standing or high-angled crouching buttons

mai-crouch-light-punch.name: Crouching Light Punch
mai-crouch-light-punch.notes_sc: Chains into 5LP/2LP/2LK; longest range of Mai's light normals; topmost hurtbox on frames 1-6 is vulnerable only to aerial strikes (preventing its use as an anti-air)

mai-crouch-medium-punch.name: Crouching Medium Punch
notes: 2 frames of recovery added when the attack misses
mai-crouch-medium-punch.notes_sc: 2f extra recovery on whiff; good range for a cancelable 6f medium; the forward movement makes it riskier to whiff than similar attacks

mai-crouch-heavy-punch.name: Crouching Heavy Punch
mai-crouch-heavy-punch.notes_sc: 2 hits; first hit has great hitbox priority for counter-poking and pulls the opponent closer on hit/block; useful as an approach or combo extension with Drive Rush; Counter-hit/Punish Counter advantage carries through both hits

mai-crouch-medium-kick.name: Crouching Medium Kick
mai-crouch-medium-kick.notes_sc: Cancelable low poke with decent range; useful Counter-hit conversion after 5LP at ranges where 2MP whiffs; has an extended hurtbox on whiff until the end of recovery; DR cancel is delayed until after active frames

mai-crouch-heavy-kick.name: Crouching Heavy Kick
notes: 1 frame of recovery added when blocked

mai-jump-light-punch.name: Jumping Light Punch
mai-jump-light-punch.notes_sc: Fastest air-to-air option with good horizontal hitbox priority

mai-jump-medium-punch.name: Jumping Medium Punch
notes: Forces a juggle state when hitting a mid-air opponent
mai-jump-medium-punch.notes_sc: Puts airborne opponents into limited juggle state; strong air-to-air hitbox; lower body projectile invuln 7-22f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

mai-jump-heavy-punch.name: Jumping Heavy Punch
mai-jump-heavy-punch.notes_sc: Good hitbox for vertical approach; lower body projectile invuln 9-23f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

mai-jump-light-kick.name: Jumping Light Kick
mai-jump-light-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up; can be used as a fuzzy instant overhead (except vs. Blanka)

mai-jump-medium-kick.name: Jumping Medium Kick
mai-jump-medium-kick.notes_sc: Cross-up; lower body projectile invuln 7-20f except around attack hitbox (cannot be used to avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

mai-jump-heavy-kick.name: Jumping Heavy Kick
mai-jump-heavy-kick.notes_sc: Most horizontal range of Mai's jump-ins; lower body projectile invuln 10-23f (if used immediately on the way up, can avoid the 2nd hit of JP's OD Amnesia bombs)

mai-senkotsu-uchi.name: Senkotsu Uchi
mai-senkotsu-uchi.notes_sc: Fast overhead that can combo into lights with perfect meaty timing; spike knockdown vs. airborne opponents

mai-l-musasabi-no-mai.name: Musasabi no Mai (Light)
notes: Can transition to Air Chou Hissatsu Shinobi Bachi between frames 1 and 40

mai-boosted-l-musasabi-no-mai.name: [Boosted] Musasabi no Mai (Light)
mai-boosted-l-musasabi-no-mai.name_ja: [強化版] ムササビの舞 (弱)

notes: Can transition to Air Chou Hissatsu Shinobi Bachi between frames 1 and 40

mai-h-musasabi-no-mai.name: Musasabi no Mai (Heavy)

notes: Can transition to Air Chou Hissatsu Shinobi Bachi between frames 1 and 40

notes: Can transition to Air Chou Hissatsu Shinobi Bachi between frames 1 and 40

mai-midare-kachousen.name: Midare Kachousen
notes: Trajectory changes on hit or block
After the trajectory change, properties change to 'High - Mid-air Projectile'
After the trajectory and property change, does not hit against moves invincible to mid-air projectiles

invuln.projectile: After the trajectory and property change, does not hit against moves invincible to mid-air projectiles

mai-midare-kachousen.notes_sc: Throws an additional 2-hit projectile with OD priority (does not dissipate on contact with strike hitboxes); on contact with the opponent, the fan splits into 2 additional projectiles and bounces up before hitting on the way down; the two falling fans bounce at staggered heights, with the second hitting much later than the first; if 1 hit of the first Midare Kachousen is dissipated then the low bouncing fan does not appear; puts opponents into a limited juggle state if the initial Midare Kachousen fan connects, while the falling fans keep the opponent in grounded hitstun; () refers to damage/hitstun values of the bouncing follow-ups on hit or block; any hit that connects after the follow-up bounce overwrites the fan's hitstun/blockstun; Mai has an extended hurtbox vulnerable to strikes on her arm/head (28-79f); the listed frame advantage assumes Mai is at point blank range; there are small interruptible gaps on block before Midare Kachousen or the bouncing follow-ups connect; the exact gaps between falling fans varies greatly depending on spacing, standing/crouching, and character height; the bouncing fan does not trigger proximity guard (allowing back walk) and counts as an air attack (allows anti-air invuln moves to pass through)

mai-boosted-midare-kachousen.name: [Boosted] Midare Kachousen
notes: Trajectory changes on hit or block
After the trajectory change, properties change to 'High - Mid-air Projectile'
After the trajectory and property change, does not hit against moves invincible to mid-air projectiles

invuln.projectile: After the trajectory and property change, does not hit against moves invincible to mid-air projectiles

mai-boosted-midare-kachousen.notes_sc: Throws an additional 2-hit projectile with OD priority (does not dissipate on contact with strike hitboxes); on contact with the opponent, the fan splits into 2 additional projectiles and bounces up before hitting on the way down; the two falling fans bounce at staggered heights, with the second hitting much later than the first; if 1 hit of the first Midare Kachousen is dissipated then the low bouncing fan does not appear; puts opponents into a limited juggle state if the initial Midare Kachousen fan connects, while the falling fans keep the opponent in grounded hitstun; () refers to damage/hitstun values of the bouncing follow-ups on hit or block; any hit that connects after the follow-up bounce overwrites the fan's hitstun/blockstun; Mai has an extended hurtbox vulnerable to strikes on her arm/head (28-79f); the listed frame advantage assumes Mai is at point blank range; there are small interruptible gaps on block before Midare Kachousen or the bouncing follow-ups connect; the exact gaps between falling fans varies greatly depending on spacing, standing/crouching, and character height; the bouncing fan does not trigger proximity guard (allowing back walk) and counts as an air attack (allows anti-air invuln moves to pass through)

mai-forward-throw.name: Forward Throw
notes: Can be broken by pressing LP+LK

mai-back-throw.name: Back Throw
notes: Can be broken by pressing LP+LK

mai-air-throw.name: Air Throw
notes: Can be broken by pressing LP+LK

mai-hien-ren-kyaku-2.name: Hien Ren Kyaku (2)
notes: Forces the opponent into a standing position

mai-hien-ren-kyaku-3.name: Hien Ren Kyaku (3)
notes: Can transition to Musasabi no Mai (Overdrive included) or Air Chou Hissatsu Shinobi Bachi between frames 18 and 33

mai-hoshi-kujaku-1.name: Hoshi Kujaku (1)
notes: Forces the opponent into a standing position

mai-hoshi-kujaku-2.name: Hoshi Kujaku (2)
notes: Fully invincible from frames 10 - 58 on hit only

invuln.full: Fully invincible from frames 10 - 58 on hit only

mai-kagerou-no-mai.name: Kagerou no Mai
notes: Invincible to strikes and throws between frames 1 - 8
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter

invuln.strike_throw: Invincible to strikes and throws between frames 1 - 8

mai-kagerou-no-mai.notes_sc: Grants Mai 5 flame stocks on the last frame of recovery; flame stocks enhance special moves and SA1/SA2 (stocks do not carry over between rounds); short horizontal range but has a good anti-air hitbox that hits on both sides; KD advantage refers to grounded hit (higher advantage vs. airborne but also variable); 8-hit projectile, but Perfect Parry only incurs 1 recovery frame per hit (allows the opponent to interrupt between hits after one Perfect Parry); this projectile hitbox can cause it to lose to projectile invuln options when used as a reversal; on hit/block, the last 2 projectile hits have 1 active frame each, occurring consecutively (the remaining frames are converted to "recovery frames")

mai-boosted-kagerou-no-mai.name: [Boosted] Kagerou no Mai
notes: Invincible to strikes and throws between frames 1 - 6
Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter

invuln.strike_throw: Invincible to strikes and throws between frames 1 - 6

mai-boosted-kagerou-no-mai.notes_sc: Grants Mai 5 flame stocks on the last frame of recovery; flame stocks enhance special moves and SA1/SA2 (stocks do not carry over between rounds); short horizontal range but has a good anti-air hitbox that hits on both sides; KD advantage refers to grounded hit (higher advantage vs. airborne but also variable); 8-hit projectile, but Perfect Parry only incurs 1 recovery frame per hit (allows the opponent to interrupt between hits after one Perfect Parry); this projectile hitbox can cause it to lose to projectile invuln options when used as a reversal; on hit/block, the last 2 projectile hits have 1 active frame each, occurring consecutively (the remaining frames are converted to "recovery frames")

mai-chou-hissatsu-shinobi-bachi.name: Chou Hissatsu Shinobi Bachi
notes: Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 40%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
mai-chou-hissatsu-shinobi-bachi.notes_sc: 11f extra recovery on block; solid range, but a short invincibility window that makes it difficult to use against slower projectiles; deals less damage from long range due to earlier hits whiffing; good reversal and juggle ender; generally should not be canceled into from OD specials (214PP: manually juggle afterward, 236KK: juggle j.MP > Air SA2)

mai-boosted-chou-hissatsu-shinobi-bachi.name: [Boosted] Chou Hissatsu Shinobi Bachi
notes: Minimum guaranteed damage is set to 40%
Values do not change on counter or Punish Counter
mai-boosted-chou-hissatsu-shinobi-bachi.notes_sc: 11f extra recovery on block; solid range, but a short invincibility window that makes it difficult to use against slower projectiles; deals less damage from long range due to earlier hits whiffing; good reversal and juggle ender; generally should not be canceled into from OD specials (214PP: manually juggle afterward, 236KK: juggle j.MP > Air SA2)

mai-shiranui-ryuu-enbu-ada-zakura.name: Shiranui Ryuu: Enbu Ada Zakura
notes: Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
* Can only be canceled into from special moves
mai-shiranui-ryuu-enbu-ada-zakura.notes_sc: 1st hit causes cinematic; non-cinematic version does less damage (varies based on how many hits connect), but can juggle into 623K to recover some lost damage; cinematic time regenerates ~2 Drive bars for Mai; 3f startup when canceled from 236K/KK only; slower startup makes it risky as a reversal (many light normals can be safe with meaty timing)

mai-boosted-shiranui-ryuu-enbu-ada-zakur.name: [Boosted] Shiranui Ryuu: Enbu Ada Zakura
mai-boosted-shiranui-ryuu-enbu-ada-zakur.name_ja: [強化版] 不知火流：炎舞徒桜
notes: Values do not change when the attack lands as a counter or Punish Counter
* Can only be canceled into from special moves
mai-boosted-shiranui-ryuu-enbu-ada-zakur.notes_sc: 1st hit causes cinematic; non-cinematic version does less damage (varies based on how many hits connect), but can juggle into 623K to recover some lost damage; cinematic time regenerates ~2 Drive bars for Mai; 3f startup when canceled from 236K/KK only; slower startup makes it risky as a reversal (many light normals can be safe with meaty timing)
